The '''Bonadan alloy heavy suit''' or '''Bonadan-alloy armor''' was a type of light [[Armor/Legends|armor]] that saw use in [[3956 BBY]], during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. The armor was created by the emerging society on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bonadan/Legends|Bonadan]] and was well-known for its strength. Along with other small arms, the armor was exported to fund Bonadan's exploration of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. It was constructed with a [[Bonadan alloy|unique alloy]] created on the [[Spaceport/Legends|port]] world, and was owned by several [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] throughout the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. The armor continued to be available several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later in [[3951 BBY]], during the [[Dark Wars]], when it was worn by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Carth Onasi]]. 

==Description==
The Bonadan alloy heavy suit,<ref name="KOTOR" /> also called Bonadan-alloy armor, was constructed using [[Bonadan alloy]], a unique and heavy alloy created on the industrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bonadan/Legends|Bonadan]] that increased the strength of the [[Armor/Legends|armor]].<ref name="Campaign" /> Despite its heavy component materials, the [[Clothing/Legends|suit]] was considered light and could be worn by those with no training in the use of heavier armor types.<ref name="KOTOR" /> The alloy suit was known for its defensive strength, including against slashing [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapons]].<ref name="Campaign" /> During the [[Jedi Civil War]], the armor cost between 900 and 1,350 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]],<ref name="KOTOR" /> although the price had increased by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the subsequent [[Dark Wars]], costing from 1,700 up to 2,040 credits. By this time, the armor was high-quality enough to be capable of enhancement with different types of [[alloy overlay]]s and [[underlay]]s.<ref name="TSL" />

The suit was comprised of a large [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Metal/Legends|metal]] chest plate with tan cloth underneath, as well as bright red sleeves and pants with black stripes down the side. The complete suit also had red [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], black [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and a beige [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]] with a single red [[Holster/Legends|holster]] attached.<ref name="KOTOR" />

==History==
{{Quote|Yes, let's get right to business. I carry only the rarest of items. Expensive, yes– but worth every credit! Weapons, armor, equipment: I carry a little of everything.|translated=1|Mika Dorin, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:Mikadorin.jpg|thumb|left|215px|Mika Dorin (pictured) offered exclusive stock to Revan.]]
The Bonadan alloy heavy suit was owned by [[Merchant/Legends|vendors]] and used throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the Jedi Civil War, a conflict between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. The armor was developed by an emerging industrial society on<ref name="KOTOR" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Spaceport/Legends|port]] planet<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Bonadan. The densely populated world, despite having banned [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]],<ref name="Campaign" /> produced and exported small arms<ref name="KOTOR" /> and their renowned high-quality armor<ref name="Campaign" /> in order to finance the exploration of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] undertaken by the society on Bonadan.<ref name="KOTOR" />

In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="Date1">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' in [[3956 BBY]].</ref> a set of the armor was owned by<ref name="KOTOR" /> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Kebla Yurt]],<ref name="Gender1">Game files from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' establish [[Kebla Yurt]] as having a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] head model.</ref> who ran an [[Kebla Yurt's Equipment Emporium|Equipment Emporium]] in the [[Upper City]] of the Outer Rim planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. Unlike much of her other stock, the armor was not confiscated by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] during their [[Capture of Taris|occupation]] and quarantine of the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
That [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="Date1" /> another set was owned by [[Mika Dorin]], a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]] of [[The Drunk Side]], a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] in the small [[Dreshdae]] [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] on the Outer Rim Sith planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. The armor was part of the specialty stock Dorin wanted to offer to the [[Amnesia/Legends|amnesiac]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who had been [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and recently discovered<ref name="KOTOR" /> his<ref name="Campaign" /> past. After Revan's [[Skirmish aboard the Leviathan|escape]] from<ref name="KOTOR" /> the Sith [[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruiser]]<ref name="Campaign" /> [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], Dorin sent a business partner,<ref name="KOTOR" /> the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] male [[Ziagrom]],<ref name="Gender2">Game files from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' establish [[Ziagrom]] as having a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] head model.</ref> to approach Revan and inform him that Dorin was willing to engage in trade with the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|redeemed]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] using their exclusive premium stock.<ref name="KOTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

The armor could still be found in the stock of many vendors<ref name="TSL" /> half a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] later, in [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="Date2">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' in [[3951 BBY]]</ref> during the Dark Wars that followed the Jedi Civil War and saw the [[First Jedi Purge|purge]] of most remaining Jedi. One set of the armor was worn by Revan's old companion and war hero-turned-[[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] [[Carth Onasi]] during his time observing the Jedi [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|Exile]] [[Meetra Surik]] and during incumbent Dark Lord of the Sith [[Darth Nihilus]]'s [[Battle of Telos IV|attack]] on [[Citadel Station]] [[Orbit/Legends|over]] his [[Homeworld/Legends|home planet]] [[Telos IV]].<ref name="TSL" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:BonadanAlloyHeavySuitIcon.png|thumb|right|Menu icon]]
The Bonadan alloy heavy suit first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', a [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] developed by [[BioWare]]. It was found in the stock of Kebla Yurt and Mika Dorin, both vendors whose interactions do not affect plot or alignment. Mika Dorin's stock is not accessible until the player escapes the ''Leviathan'' and learns Revan's identity, after which they will be approached by Ziagrom if they visit Korriban. [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]], published in 2003 by [[Prima Games]], misspells Bonadan as "Bonadon," calls the armor suit a combat vest on one instance, and incorrectly states that Dorin sells the Bonadan alloy heavy suit for 2,500 credits.<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[2008]] ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'', a supplement to their ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', identifies the suit as Bonadan-alloy armor.<ref name="Campaign" />

The [[2004]] sequel video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', in which the armor appears, uses randomization when determining the stock of vendors, rewards, or loot in containers or remains. As such, the Bonadan alloy heavy suit has a chance to be found on multiple planets, but is not guaranteed on any. The individuals with a chance of selling or rewarding the armor include the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Samhan Dobo]] on Citadel Station, the Duros [[Oondar]] and Twi'lek [[Fassa (Twi'lek)|Fassa]] on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta's]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Margar]] and Human [[Gegorran]] in the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] on the planet [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Kex]] on Onderon's moon [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], and the Rodian [[Adum Larp]] in the [[Matale estate|headquarters]] of the [[Khoonda]] government on the planet [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. The armor only has a chance to be rewarded by [[Nar Shaddaa Docks|dock]] master Fassa if the player completes the "Fassa's [[Freighter/Legends|Freighters]]" quest while levels ten or eleven.<ref name="TSL" />

The Bonadan alloy heavy suit is identical in appearance to that of the [[Echani shield suit]] that appears in ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'', but game files establish that Carth Onasi wore the former during his appearances.<ref name="TSL" />
